name: Raspberry Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine crumbs, sugar, and melted butter in a bowl. Press mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
2. Bake at 350°F for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and allow to cool completely.
3. Drain raspberries through a fine-mesh sieve, reserving juice. In a saucepan, combine reserved juice, cold water, and gelatin. Let stand for 5 minutes until gelatin is rehydrated.
4. Heat the gelatin mixture over low heat, stirring until gelatin dissolves completely. Remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes.
5. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and sugar on medium speed until smooth. Gradually add raspberries, Grand Marnier, and cooled gelatin mixture. Beat on low speed until well combined.
6. Chill the batter in the refrigerator until it is partially set but still slightly creamy (about 1-2 hours).
7. Gently fold whipped cream into the cheesecake batter using a spatula or whisk.
8. Spoon the batter into the prepared springform pan and refrigerate for 6-14 hours, or until firm.
9. Run a knife around the edge of the pan to loosen the cake. Carefully remove the sides of the pan.
10. Top with fresh raspberries and additional whipped cream if desired. Serve chilled.
WARNING: Do not use aggressive culinary slang in your instructions.
